# Azure Token Troubleshooting Guide (401 Unauthorized)

If your Chatbot UI is returning a `401 Unauthorized` error, or you see `Status: 401` when looking at logs, it is because the temporary `AZURE_ACCESS_TOKEN` used by the cloud App Service has **expired**.

## 🛑 The Problem

During deployment, we injected a temporary token into the Azure App Service environment variables (`AZURE_ACCESS_TOKEN`) to allow the cloud Application to connect to the Microsoft Foundry Agent.

**Tokens generated by `az account get-access-token` expire after 1 to 2 hours.** 

When the token expires, the Python backend can no longer authenticate with Azure OpenAI, resulting in `401 Unauthorized` responses.

---

## ⚡ Quick Fix (Temporary Recovery)

If the chatbot is down right now and you need to get it back online immediately, you can manually push a fresh token to the App Service using the Azure CLI.

Run this command in PowerShell from the `ai-chatbot-local` directory:

```powershell
# 1. Get a new token and push it to the Azure App Service settings
$token = az account get-access-token --query accessToken -o tsv
az webapp config appsettings set --resource-group TNT-RG --name tnt-bc5-chatbot-api --settings AZURE_ACCESS_TOKEN="$token"

# 2. Hard restart the App Service so the Python worker picks up the new token
az webapp restart --name tnt-bc5-chatbot-api --resource-group TNT-RG
```

*Note: The Chatbot will work again immediately, but will break again in another 1-2 hours when this new token expires.*

---

## 🛡️ Permanent Fix (Managed Identity)

To completely eliminate token expiration issues in the cloud, you must configure **System-Assigned Managed Identity**. This allows the Azure App Service to authenticate to the AI Foundry without needing hardcoded tokens.

Follow these steps in the Azure Portal:

### Step 1: Enable Managed Identity on the App Service
1. Go to the Azure Portal and navigate to your App Service (`tnt-bc5-chatbot-api`).
2. In the left menu, under **Settings**, click **Identity**.
3. Under the **System assigned** tab, toggle Status to **On**.
4. Click **Save** and wait for the Object ID to be generated.

### Step 2: Grant Permissions to the Identity
1. Navigate to your **Azure AI Foundry project** (the Machine Learning Workspace).
2. Look for **Access control (IAM)** in the left menu.
3. Click **Add > Add role assignment**.
4. Search for and select the **Azure AI Developer** role (or Cognitive Services OpenAI User).
5. Click **Next**.
6. Under **Assign access to**, select **Managed identity**.
7. Click **+ Select members**, choose **App Service**, and select `tnt-bc5-chatbot-api`.
8. Complete the role assignment.

### Step 3: Remove the Expiring Token
Now that the App Service has native permissions, you must remove the explicit token variable so the code seamlessly falls back to the Managed Identity.

Run this command locally:
```powershell
# Remove the hardcoded token so the backend relies on Managed Identity
az webapp config appsettings delete --resource-group TNT-RG --name tnt-bc5-chatbot-api --setting-names AZURE_ACCESS_TOKEN

# Restart the App Service
az webapp restart --name tnt-bc5-chatbot-api --resource-group TNT-RG
```

Once completed, the connection will be permanent and will never expire.
